rationale_for_baseline_tier: New acute decompensated left ventricular failure with dyspnoea at rest, three-pillow orthopnoea/PND, resting tachypnoea and hypoxia (SpO2 94%) and early pulmonary oedema. The patient needs same-day hospital assessment for oximetry, ECG, troponin, chest X-ray, bloods/BNP and echocardiography, plus intravenous diuresis — none of which telehealth can deliver, and the valve/heart-failure cause cannot be confirmed or excluded remotely. This exceeds routine or urgent GP care (T4, emergency department, same-day). It is not an immediate lights-and-sirens T5 while the patient is stable sitting upright without flash oedema, but the threshold to T5 is low and is safety-netted below.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Sudden severe breathlessness at rest, unable to speak in full sentences, or worsening despite sitting upright
escalation_edges.rationale: Rapidly worsening rest breathlessness signals decompensation into acute pulmonary oedema requiring emergency ambulance care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If your breathing suddenly gets much worse, you can't speak a full sentence, or it keeps getting worse even sitting up — call 000 for an ambulance straight away.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Coughing up pink, frothy sputum (flash pulmonary oedema)
escalation_edges.rationale: Pink frothy sputum is acute cardiogenic pulmonary oedema — an immediate threat to life needing emergency transport and treatment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you cough up frothy pink spit, call 000 immediately and tell them your lungs are filling with fluid.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New chest pain, pressure or tightness
escalation_edges.rationale: Chest pain raises acute coronary syndrome, which can precipitate or accompany decompensation and requires emergency assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you get any chest pain, pressure or tightness, call 000 straight away — don't wait.

escalation_edges.rationale: Syncope or cyanosis signals critical cardiorespiratory compromise requiring immediate emergency care.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If you feel faint and pass out, or your lips or fingers turn blue, call 000 immediately.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Ongoing rest breathlessness, orthopnoea and swelling without acute crisis features
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Same day — do not wait for a routine appointment
escalation_edges.rationale: The baseline decompensated picture already warrants same-day emergency-department assessment for oximetry, ECG, imaging, bloods and diuresis, and to define the cause.
escalation_edges.communication_template: Because you're breathless at rest and can't lie flat, you need to be seen in person today at the emergency department — please arrange to go now.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult with a first, previously undiagnosed decompensation and limited physiological reserve
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Lower the threshold to escalate; do not attribute breathlessness to ageing or deconditioning.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Spouse present and able to assist and transport
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: The carer can help arrange same-day transport and monitor for the 000 triggers; confirm they know exactly when to call an ambulance.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: You need to be assessed in person today — go to the emergency department for a heart tracing, chest X-ray, blood tests and a heart scan, and treatment to clear the fluid. Until you're seen, sit upright and rest. Call 000 for an ambulance immediately if your breathing suddenly worsens at rest, you cough up pink frothy spit, you get chest pain, or you feel faint.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Attend the emergency department today; call 000 for any of the emergency triggers above
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: The emergency department and 000 are available 24 hours a day — do not wait for GP hours if you are breathless at rest.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Provide the GP and cardiologist with the presenting congestive symptoms (orthopnoea, PND, rest dyspnoea, ankle oedema), the ED findings and results, and the plan for urgent echocardiography and cardiology/valve assessment.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating decompensated heart failure with rest dyspnoea and hypoxia as a routine or prompt GP problem, or as a self-managed chest infection.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: The patient tips into flash pulmonary oedema and respiratory failure at home without emergency care, risking death.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Directing to urgent same-day in-person care without an emergency-department pathway is acceptable only if strong 000 safety-netting is given; without it, hospital-level tests and IV diuresis are delayed.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: Calling an emergency ambulance for a patient currently stable sitting upright is over-triage, though defensible given resting hypoxia and far less harmful than under-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot auscultate the murmur/crackles, measure vitals, or obtain ECG/CXR/echo, so neither the heart-failure severity nor the valve cause can be confirmed or the dangerous mimics (ACS, pneumonia) excluded remotely.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the reported congestive syndrome as decompensated heart failure until proven otherwise and escalate for same-day in-person assessment with clear emergency safety-netting.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Guessing a specific valve diagnosis remotely, or being falsely reassured by denied chest pain and normal-sounding voice to under-triage to a routine review or home self-management.
